title: Human Life
content:
آ آ آ آ What mortal, when he saw,
آ آ آ آ Lifeï؟½s voyage done, his heavenly Friend,
آ آ آ آ Could ever yet dare tell him fearlessly:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½I have kept uninfringï؟½d my natureï؟½s law;
آ آ آ آ The inly-written chart thou gayest me
آ آ آ آ To guide me, I have steerï؟½d by to the endï؟½?

آ آ آ آ Ah! let us make no claim
آ آ آ آ On lifeï؟½s incognizable sea
آ آ آ آ To too exact a steering of our way!
آ آ آ آ Let us not fret and fear to miss our aim
آ آ آ آ If some fair coast has lured us to make stay,
آ آ آ آ Or some friend hailï؟½d us to keep company !

آ آ آ آ Aye, we would each fain drive
آ آ آ آ At random, and not steer by rule!
آ آ آ آ Weakness! and worse, weakness bestowï؟½d in vain!
آ آ آ آ Winds from our side the unsuiting consort rive,
آ آ آ آ We rush by coasts where we had lief remain;
آ آ آ آ Man cannot, though he would, live chanceï؟½s fool.

آ آ آ آ No! as the foaming swathe
آ آ آ آ Of torn-up water, on the main,
آ آ آ آ Falls heavily away with long-drawn roar
آ آ آ آ On either side the black deep-furrowï؟½d path
آ آ آ آ Cut by an onward-labouring vesselï؟½s prore,
آ آ آ آ And never touches the ship-side again;

آ آ آ آ Even so we leave behind,
آ آ آ آ As, charterï؟½d by some unknown Powers,
آ آ آ آ We stem across the sea of life by night,
آ آ آ آ The joys which were not for our use designï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ The friends to whom we had no natural right,
آ آ آ آ The homes that were not destined to be ours.
meaning:
About: Matthew Arnold (24 December 1822 ï؟½ 15 April 1888) was an English poet and cultural critic, who worked as an inspector of schools. 
He was the son of Thomas Arnold, the famed headmaster of Rugby School, and brother to Tom Arnold, literary professor, and William Delafield Arnold, novelist and colonial administrator.
